message.content: Adversaries may exploit alternate authentication material, such as password hashes, Kerberos tickets, and application access tokens, to move laterally within an environment and bypass standard access controls. These materials are generated after successful authentication and are cached by systems to avoid repeated authentication prompts. However, if compromised, they can be used to authenticate without needing the plaintext password or additional factors. This technique can lead to significant security breaches, as seen with APT29's use of forged SAML tokens to bypass MFA and access enterprise cloud services. Common exploitation methods include stealing cached credentials through Credential Access techniques. Mitigation strategies involve regularly clearing cached credentials, implementing robust monitoring for unusual authentication activities, and employing multi-factor authentication (MFA) to reduce reliance on single authentication factors.
